steps.title: Spot the two-part requirement
steps.content: The strongest clue is the explicit requirement: the answer must both make a claim about Iberian orca behavior AND support it with evidence from the notes. No partial credit—both parts are essential.

steps.title: Apply the requirement to the choices
steps.content: Choice A makes a clear causal claim—destructive behavior results from understimulation—and supports it directly by linking reduced hunting time to increased vessel interactions. The other choices either lack a claim about orca behavior (C is purely descriptive), address a different topic entirely (B focuses on TAST), or contradict the notes about cause and effect (D reverses the tuna-hunting relationship). That matches choice A — A is correct.
